title: oxypolis filiformis subsp. greenmanii
intro: Oxypolis filiformis subsp. greenmanii, also known as Greenman's oxypolis or Greenman's sweetroot, is a plant species in the carrot family (Apiaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Oxypolis filiformis subsp. greenmanii include its ability to support the immune system, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being. Oxypolis filiformis subsp. greenmanii is typically prepared as a tincture or decoction for medicinal use, and its roots and rhizomes are often used in herbal remedies.
